title: Vignea × haussknechtii
taxonomy: Vignea × haussknechtii is a hybrid species of grapevine. It is classified within the genus Vitis and is often considered a parent species of the cultivated grapevine. The classification is based on its characteristics, such as its morphology and genetic makeup. This species is closely related to other Vitis species and is used in research to better understand the genetics of grapevine evolution. Vignea × haussknechtii is often found in the wild and has been studied for its potential use in breeding new grapevine varieties.

intro: Vignea × haussknechtii, also known as Vigna haussknechtii, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly referred to as the hairy hairy bean or hairy kidney bean. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the reduction of blood sugar levels and the alleviation of symptoms associated with diabetes and hyperlipidemia. The bioactive constituents of Vigna haussknechtii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic properties. In traditional medicine, Vigna haussknechtii is often used to prepare herbal decoctions, teas, and infusions, as well as extracts and capsules, to support cardiovascular and metabolic health.
